tex:
A grocer sells potatoes, mushrooms and carrots.

(a) A customer buys 3 kg of mushrooms at \$1.04 per kg and 4 kg of carrots at \$1.28 per kg.

Calculate the total cost.

(b) In one week, the ratio of the masses of vegetables sold by the grocer is

\centerline{potatoes : mushrooms : carrots = 11 : 8 : 6.}

(i) (c) Work out the mass of mushrooms sold as a percentage of the total mass.

(d) The total mass of potatoes, mushrooms and carrots sold is 1500 kg.

Find the mass of carrots the grocer sells this week.

(e) The profit the grocer makes selling 1 kg of carrots is \$0.75 .

Find the total profit the grocer makes selling carrots this week.

(f) On the last day of the week, the grocer reduces the price of 1 kg of potatoes by 8\% to \$1.15 .

Calculate the original price of 1 kg of potatoes.

(g) The grocer buys 620 kg of onions, correct to the nearest 20 kg.
He packs them into bags each containing 5 kg of onions, correct to the nearest 1 kg.

Calculate the upper bound for the number of bags of onions that he packs.

\begin{InsertScreenShot}

\end{InsertScreenShot}

$A$, $B$, $C$ and $D$ are points on a circle.
$ADX$ and $BCX$ are straight lines.
Angle $BAD = x^\circ$ and angle $DCX = y^\circ$.

(a) Explain why $x = y$.
Give a geometrical reason for each statement you make.

(b) Show that triangle $ABX$ is similar to triangle $CDX$.

(c) $AD = 15$ cm, $DX = 9$ cm and $CX = 12$ cm.

(i) (d) Find $BC$.

(e) Complete the statement.

The ratio area of triangle $ABX$ : area of triangle $CDX = $  : 1.

(a) The table shows information about the marks gained by each of 10 students in a test.

\begin{tabular}{|l|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
Mark & 15 & 16 & 17 & 18 & 19 & 20 \\
\hline
Frequency & 4 & 1 & 2 & 1 & 0 & 2 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
(i) (b) Calculate the range.

(c) Calculate the mean.

(d) Find the median.

(e) Write down the mode.

(f) Paulo’s mean mark for 7 homework tasks is 17.
After completing the 8th task, his mean mark is 17.5 .

Calculate Paulo’s mark for the 8th task.

(g) The table shows the percentage scored by each of 100 students in their final exam.

\begin{tabular}{|l|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
Percentage ($p$) & $0 < p \leqslant 30$ & $30 < p \leqslant 50$ & $50 < p \leqslant 60$ & $60 < p \leqslant 70$ & $70 < p \leqslant 100$ \\
\hline
Frequency & 12 & 18 & 35 & 20 & 15 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
On the grid, draw a histogram to show this information.

\begin{InsertScreenShot}

\end{InsertScreenShot}

The diagram shows a pyramid with a square base $BCDE$.
The diagonals $CE$ and $BD$ intersect at $M$, and the vertex $F$ is directly above $M$.
$BE = 12$ cm and $FM = 9$ cm.

(i) (b) Calculate the volume of the pyramid.
[The volume, $V$, of a pyramid with base area $A$ and height $h$ is $V = \frac{1}{3} Ah$.]

(c) Calculate the total surface area of the pyramid.

(d) \begin{InsertScreenShot}

\end{InsertScreenShot}

The diagram shows a toy made from a cone and a hemisphere.
The base radius of the cone and the radius of the hemisphere are both $r$ cm.
The slant height of the cone is $3r$ cm.

The total surface area of the toy is 304 cm$^2$.

Calculate the value of $r$.

[The curved surface area, $A$, of a cone with radius $r$ and slant height $l$ is $A = \pi rl$.]
[The curved surface area, $A$, of a sphere with radius $r$ is $A = 4 \pi r^2$.]

(a) (i) (b) Factorise.
$$x^2 - x - 12$$

(c) Simplify.
$$\frac{x^2 - 16}{x^2 - x - 12}$$

(d) Simplify.
$$(2x - 3)^2 - (x + 1)^2$$

(e) Write as a single fraction in its simplest form.
$$\frac{2x + 4}{x + 1} - \frac{x}{x - 3}$$

(f) Expand and simplify.
$$(x - 3) (x - 5) (2x + 1)$$

(g) Solve the simultaneous equations.
You must show all your working.

\begin{align*}
x - 3y &= 13 \\
2x^2 - 9y &= 116
\end{align*}

\begin{InsertScreenShot}

\end{InsertScreenShot}

The diagram shows triangle $ABC$ with $AB = 17.2$ cm.
Angle $ABC = 54^\circ$ and angle $ACB = 68^\circ$.

(a) Calculate $AC$.

(b) $M$ lies on $BC$ and $MC = 12.8$ cm.

Calculate $AM$.

(c) Calculate the shortest distance from $A$ to $BC$.

(a) Janna and Kamal each invest \$8000.
At the end of 12 years, they each have \$12\,800.

(i) (b) Janna invests in an account that pays simple interest at a rate of $r$\% per year.

Calculate the value of $r$.

(c) Kamal invests in an account that pays compound interest at a rate of $R$\% per year.

Calculate the value of $R$.

(d) The population of a city is growing exponentially at a rate of 1.8\% per year.
The population now is 260\,000.

Find the number of complete years from now when the population will first be more than 300\,000.

The table shows some values for $y = 2x^3 + 6x^2 - 2.5$.

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
$x$ & $-3$ & $-2.5$ & $-2$ & $-1.5$ & $-1$ & $-0.5$ & $0$ & $0.5$ & $1$ \\
\hline
$y$ & & $3.75$ & $5.5$ & $4.25$ & $1.5$ & & $-2.5$ & $-0.75$ & \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
(a) Complete the table.

(b) On the grid, draw the graph of $y = 2x^3 + 6x^2 - 2.5$ for $-3 \leqslant x \leqslant 1$.

\begin{InsertScreenShot}

\end{InsertScreenShot}

(c) By drawing a suitable line on the graph, solve the equation $2x^3 + 6x^2 = 4.5$.

(d) The equation $2x^3 + 6x^2 - 2.5 = k$ has exactly two solutions.

Write down the two possible values of $k$.

tex:
$$f(x) = \frac{1}{x}, x \neq 0 \quad g(x) = 3x - 5 \quad h(x) = 2^x$$

(a) Find.
(i) (b) $gf(2)$

(c) $g^{-1}(x)$

(d) Find in its simplest form $g(x - 2)$.

(e) Find the value of $x$ when

(i) (f) $fg(x) = 0.1$

(g) $h(x) - g(7) = 0$.

Calculate the perimeter of the remaining shaded shape.

(b) The diagram in part(a) shows the top of a cylindrical cake with a slice removed.
The volume of cake that remains is 3510 cm$^3$.

Calculate the height of the cake.
